title: pouteria howeana
intro: Pouteria howeana, also known as the Koa Haole, is a plant species native to Hawaii, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The Koa Haole has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Pouteria howeana include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or mixing the extract with other herbal remedies.
